notes: Unit-level breakdown (vocabulary, grammar, skills per unit) is included for all levels. Lessons and exercises are not included.
Recommended unit counts: Starters 10, Movers 10, Flyers 12, KET 12, PET 14 — aligned to theme coverage and vocabulary density per level.
Per-unit vocabulary arrays are sample anchors; full word lists should expand toward unitPlan.approximateVocabularyPerUnit during content authoring.
YLE levels (Starters, Movers, Flyers) align with Cambridge Young Learners exams.
KET and PET align with A2 Key for Schools and B1 Preliminary for Schools syllabi.
Vocabulary counts are cumulative targets for receptive + productive use at each level.

levels.name: Cambridge Starters
levels.examName: Cambridge English Qualifications – Pre A1 Starters

unitPlan.designNote: Pedagogical order: identity → numbers → description → concrete nouns → daily life → spaces → routines → activities → directions. Covers all 16 YLE Starters themes.

learningObjectives: Understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases.
Introduce themselves and answer simple questions about personal details.
Interact in a simple way when the other person speaks slowly and clearly.
Recognise and name common objects, animals, places, and people in pictures.
Follow short, simple instructions in classroom and play contexts.
Demonstrate willingness to communicate using words, phrases, and gestures.

wordTypes: Core verbs (be, have, go, like, play, eat, drink, see, look, listen, say, tell, open, close, put, take, run, walk, sit, stand)
Question words (what, where, who, how many, how old)
Prepositions of place (in, on, under, next to, behind, in front of)
vocabularyScope.productiveExpectation: Single words and memorised chunks; limited phrase-level production.

structures: Subject pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they
Verb be: am, is, are (affirmative, negative, questions)
Verb have got: I've got, has she got?
Present simple: like, play, eat, go (third person -s)
Imperatives: Open the door. Sit down.
Can for ability: I can swim.
There is / There are
Plural nouns: regular -s
Possessive 's: Tom's bike
Adjective + noun order: a big house
Basic question formation: What is this? Where is the cat?
Yes/No questions with be and can
Negation with be, have got, and can
notInScope: Future forms beyond simple time markers

taskTypes: Read short sentences and choose matching pictures
Read short dialogues and select answers
Read and write words and short phrases with picture support
skills: Decode familiar words in isolation and in short sentences
Understand meaning from pictures and text together
Recognise names, numbers, and key content words
Follow simple linear narratives (3–5 sentences)
Distinguish between similar words using visual context
Copy and complete single words and short phrases
readingSkills.textLength: Words, phrases, and sentences up to 10 words

taskTypes: Listen and draw lines between people and objects
Listen and write words or numbers
Listen to short conversations and tick a picture
skills: Identify key words in short, clear recordings
Understand familiar vocabulary in context
Follow simple instructions with visual support
Extract names, ages, numbers, and colours
Distinguish minimal pairs with picture options
Respond to slow, carefully articulated speech
listeningSkills.recordingFeatures: Short utterances, repetition, pauses between items

taskTypes: Write words and short phrases to describe pictures
Answer short questions with single words or phrases
skills: Form legible letters and spell familiar words
Complete words using phonics and visual cues
Produce labels and short phrases with model support
Write personal information (name, age, country)
Use capital letters and full stops in simple sentences
writingSkills.outputLength: Single words to short phrases (1–5 words)

taskTypes: Describe pictures in a picture story
Personalised questions linked to picture story
skills: Respond to formulaic questions about self and familiar topics
Name objects, people, and actions in pictures
Use memorised chunks and single words to communicate
Ask and answer very simple questions
Maintain basic interaction with examiner support
Use intelligible pronunciation of high-frequency words

units.title: Family and Friends
grammar: Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her

units.title: Numbers and Time
grammar: Question words: how many, how old

units.title: Colours and Clothes
grammar: Adjective + noun order: a red shirt

units.title: Toys and Weather
units.readingSkill: Describe Pictures with Weather and Toys

units.title: The Home
grammar: Prepositions of place: in, on, under

units.title: School
grammar: Have got: I've got a pen

units.title: Sports and Leisure
grammar: Can for ability: I can swim

units.title: Transport and Places
grammar: Prepositions: next to, behind, in front of

levels.name: Cambridge Movers

unitPlan.designNote: Builds on Starters themes; each unit introduces one Movers-specific topic cluster (health, materials, jobs, technology, holidays, shopping, world around us).

learningObjectives: Understand and use familiar everyday expressions and routine phrases.
Describe people, places, and possessions using simple language.
Handle short social exchanges with support.
Read and understand short factual texts on familiar topics.
Write simple connected text on familiar personal topics.
Understand the main point of short, clear announcements and messages.

vocabularyScope.coverage: Cambridge YLE Movers word list (A1); cumulative with Starters
wordTypes: Expanded nouns (abstract basics: idea, problem, weather conditions)
vocabularyScope.productiveExpectation: Short sentences and chained phrases; emerging paragraph awareness.

structures: All Starters grammar, consolidated
Present continuous: I am playing
Past simple: regular and common irregular verbs (went, saw, had, was/were)
Future with going to: We're going to visit grandma
Wh- questions across tenses
Object pronouns: me, him, her, them
Possessive pronouns: mine, yours
Countable/uncountable nouns with some/any
Comparatives: taller than, as ... as
Superlatives (introduction): the biggest
Adverbs of manner: quickly, slowly
Conjunctions: and, but, because, when

taskTypes: Read conversations and choose correct responses
Read and write short messages and notes
skills: Understand gist and specific detail in short texts
Infer meaning of unknown words from context
Follow chronological narrative (5–10 sentences)
Understand simple connectors (then, because)
Identify purpose of short messages (invitation, note)
Complete sentences with grammatically appropriate words
readingSkills.textLength: Sentences and short texts up to 80 words

taskTypes: Listen and write words, names, numbers
Listen to conversations and fill gaps
Listen and colour/draw with multiple instructions
skills: Understand main idea of short dialogues
Extract specific factual information
Follow multi-step instructions
Recognise attitude (happy, sad) in simple contexts
Understand time references and sequencing
Tolerate slightly faster speech with repetition
listeningSkills.recordingFeatures: Short dialogues and monologues; some background context

skills: Write simple sentences about familiar topics
Use past, present, and going-to future appropriately
Spell common irregular words correctly
Link ideas with basic connectors
Write messages with salutation and closing
Self-correct basic spelling and punctuation
writingSkills.outputLength: Sentences and short notes (20–40 words total per task)

skills: Sustain short turns on familiar topics
Describe actions, locations, and possessions
Use past tense to narrate simple events
Ask simple questions to the examiner
Compare two pictures using basic language
Maintain intelligibility with developing fluency

units.title: Daily Routines
grammar: Adverbs of frequency: always, sometimes, never

units.title: The Body and Health
grammar: Have got: I've got a headache
Should for advice: You should rest

units.title: Hobbies and Free Time
units.listeningSkill: Understand Main Idea of Short Dialogues

units.title: Holidays and Travel
grammar: Going to future: We're going to visit

units.title: Shopping and Money
grammar: Comparatives: cheaper than, more expensive than

units.title: Jobs and Work
grammar: Present simple third person: He works in a hospital
Wh- questions: Where does she work?

units.title: Materials and Objects
grammar: Made of: It's made of wood

units.title: Technology
grammar: Object pronouns: me, him, her, them

levels.name: Cambridge Flyers

unitPlan.designNote: Covers Flyers theme scope plus skill-based units (past experiences, future plans) for A2 productive and narrative competence.

learningObjectives: Understand sentences and frequently used expressions on familiar matters.
Communicate in simple, routine tasks requiring direct exchange of information.
Describe background, environment, and immediate needs in simple terms.
Understand short, simple texts including stories and factual passages.
Write short, simple notes and messages and connected text on familiar topics.
Interact with increasing independence in familiar situations.

vocabularyScope.coverage: Cambridge YLE Flyers word list (A2); cumulative with Movers
wordTypes: Phrasal verbs (get up, look for, turn on)
vocabularyScope.productiveExpectation: Connected sentences and short paragraphs; opinion and reason emerging.

structures: All Movers grammar, consolidated
Past simple: full irregular verb set (high-frequency)
Past continuous: was/were + -ing (introduction)
Present perfect: ever/never, just, already, yet (basic)
Future: will (predictions), going to (plans)
Modal verbs: must, mustn't, should, could, might
First conditional: If it rains, we will stay home
Relative clauses: who, which, that (defining)
Gerunds and infinitives: enjoy swimming, want to go
Too / enough
Question tags (introduction): isn't it?
Passive voice (introduction): is made, was built

taskTypes: Match headings or sentences to paragraphs
Read and write longer messages, emails, and stories
skills: Understand main ideas and specific detail in longer texts
Infer character feelings and simple motives
Understand chronological and cause-effect links
Identify text type and purpose
Use contextual clues for unknown vocabulary
Complete cohesive texts with discourse awareness
readingSkills.textLength: Texts up to 150 words; stories up to 200 words
readingSkills.cognitiveLevel: Literal, inferential, and simple evaluative comprehension

taskTypes: Listen and match speakers to statements
Listen and complete gaps in notes
Listen to longer conversations and answer questions
Listen for specific information in announcements
skills: Follow extended dialogues and monologues
Understand opinion and attitude in familiar contexts
Extract multiple pieces of information from one recording
Understand indirect answers and implied meaning (simple)
Track speakers in short interviews
Cope with natural pace with some redundancy

taskTypes: Write a short story from picture prompts
skills: Write connected text on familiar topics
Use range of tenses accurately in context
Organise ideas with paragraphs and linking words
Express simple opinions and reasons
Use appropriate register for messages and stories
Revise for spelling, punctuation, and basic cohesion
writingSkills.outputLength: 35–80 words per task; story up to 80 words

taskTypes: Discussion questions on preferences and experiences
skills: Sustain longer turns with connected ideas
Narrate past experiences with sequence markers
Express and justify simple opinions
Speculate about pictures (might, could)
Repair communication when meaning breaks down
Initiate and respond in collaborative tasks

units.title: Environment and Nature
grammar: Passive voice (introduction): is made, is recycled

units.title: Culture and Festivals
grammar: Past simple: irregular verbs (went, saw, had)

units.title: Media and Entertainment
grammar: Gerunds and infinitives: enjoy watching, want to see

units.title: Science and Discovery
grammar: Passive voice: was built, was invented
units.readingSkill: Use Contextual Clues for Unknown Vocabulary

units.title: Rules and Responsibilities
units.listeningSkill: Listen for Specific Information in Announcements

units.title: Health and Lifestyle
grammar: First conditional: If it rains, we will stay home
units.readingSkill: Understand Main Ideas in Longer Texts

levels.name: Cambridge KET

unitPlan.designNote: One unit per KET syllabus topic; balanced coverage of 12 A2 Key for Schools themes.

learningObjectives: Understand and use everyday expressions and basic phrases in practical contexts.
Introduce themselves and others; ask and answer questions about personal details.
Interact simply when speakers talk slowly and clearly.
Read and understand straightforward factual texts, signs, and notices.
Write short, simple messages connected to everyday situations.
Demonstrate A2-level competence across integrated skills for school and daily life.

vocabularyScope.coverage: A2 Key vocabulary topics (Cambridge English corpus, B1 threshold preparation)
wordTypes: Phrasal verbs (common set: pick up, find out, take off)
Idiomatic expressions (A2 frequency: hang out, look forward to)
vocabularyScope.productiveExpectation: Functional language for real-world tasks; short emails and notes.

structures: All Flyers grammar, consolidated and extended
Present simple and continuous (distinction)
Past simple and past continuous
Present perfect with for/since and experience
Future forms: will, going to, present continuous for arrangements
Modals: can, could, must, have to, should, need to
Zero and first conditional
Relative clauses (defining and non-defining, basic)
Passive voice: present and past simple
Reported speech (statements, basic)
Gerunds and infinitives after common verbs
Comparatives and superlatives (including less/fewer)
Quantifiers: much/many, a few/a little, all, every, no

taskTypes: Multiple choice with short texts (signs, messages, notices)
Multiple choice long text (detail and opinion)
Writing: email/message (25+ words) and picture story (35+ words)
skills: Skim for gist in signs, notices, and emails
Scan for specific information
Understand vocabulary in context and collocations
Identify writer purpose and audience
Follow argument and opinion in short articles
Complete texts demonstrating grammatical control
readingSkills.textLength: Texts 80–200 words; total reading load moderate

skills: Understand main idea and detail in announcements and conversations
Follow everyday social and transactional exchanges
Extract factual information from interviews
Understand attitude and opinion (basic)
Cope with natural speed with some repetition in parts
Spell names and places accurately from recordings
listeningSkills.recordingFeatures: Authentic pace; range of accents (primarily standard)
listeningSkills.cognitiveLevel: Detail extraction and gist in real-world contexts

taskTypes: Write an email or message (minimum 25 words)
Write a story from three picture prompts (minimum 35 words)
skills: Communicate clear information in functional writing
Use appropriate layout for emails and messages
Narrate events in sequence with basic cohesion
Control A2 grammar and vocabulary in production
Address all content points in the task rubric
Maintain intelligibility despite minor errors

taskTypes: Discussion with partner on related theme
skills: Sustain conversation on familiar topics
Ask and answer questions spontaneously
Negotiate and collaborate to reach decisions
Describe photographs with detail and speculation
Express preferences, opinions, and experiences
Use repair strategies and clarification requests

units.title: Education and Study
units.readingSkill: Skim for Gist in Signs and Notices

units.title: Health and Lifestyle
grammar: Countable / uncountable with a few / a little

units.title: Leisure and Entertainment
grammar: Phrasal verbs: pick up, find out, take off
Gerunds and infinitives after common verbs

units.title: Travel and Transport
grammar: Future forms: will, going to, present continuous for arrangements

units.title: Food and Shopping
grammar: Quantifiers: much, many, a few, a little, all, every

units.title: House and Home
grammar: Passive voice: present and past simple

grammar: Modals: can, could, must, have to

units.readingSkill: Multiple Choice with Short Texts (Signs, Messages)

levels.name: Cambridge PET

unitPlan.designNote: 14 units to reduce vocabulary density vs KET; extends all KET themes plus B1-specific topics (global issues, formal writing, opinions).

learningObjectives: Understand main points of clear standard input on familiar matters.
Deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling in an English-speaking area.
Produce simple connected text on familiar and personal interest topics.
Describe experiences, events, dreams, hopes, and ambitions with brief reasons.
Explain opinions and plans; narrate stories and accounts of experiences.
Demonstrate B1 threshold competence for academic and social participation.

vocabularyScope.coverage: B1 Preliminary vocabulary topics; Cambridge English B1 lexicon
themes: Science and technology in daily life
wordTypes: Discourse markers (although, however, moreover, in addition)
Academic vocabulary (B1 subset): analyse, compare, describe, explain
vocabularyScope.productiveExpectation: Paragraph-level writing and extended speaking turns with justification.

structures: All KET grammar, consolidated and extended
Present perfect vs past simple (distinction)
Past perfect (introduction)
Future continuous and future perfect (recognition)
Modals: must, can't, may, might, ought to, needn't
Second conditional: If I had money, I would travel
Mixed conditionals (basic recognition)
Passive voice: all simple tenses; modal passives
Reported speech: questions and commands
Relative clauses (full range of pronouns)
Participle clauses (introduction): Having finished, ...
Wish and regret: I wish I had ...
Indirect questions: Do you know where ...?
Verb patterns: suggest doing, advise someone to

taskTypes: Multiple choice signs and short texts
Multiple matching (five people to eight texts)
Writing: email (100 words) and article/story (100 words)
skills: Understand detail, opinion, and attitude in longer texts
Identify organisational features and cohesive devices
Infer meaning of low-frequency vocabulary from context
Evaluate purpose, audience, and tone
Follow extended narrative and argument
Complete texts demonstrating B1 lexical and grammatical range
readingSkills.textLength: Texts 150–350 words; gapped text up to 400 words

skills: Understand extended speech at natural pace
Identify speaker attitude, opinion, and purpose
Follow radio-style programmes and announcements
Understand implied meaning and indirect agreement
Extract paraphrased information (not exact words)
Process a range of accents in standard English
listeningSkills.recordingFeatures: Authentic speed; minimal repetition; varied contexts

taskTypes: Write an email in response to input (100 words)
Write an article or story from prompt (100 words)
skills: Produce coherent paragraphs on familiar topics
Use range of linking devices and paragraph structure
Express and justify opinions with supporting reasons
Adapt register to audience and text type
Narrate and describe with appropriate detail
Self-edit for accuracy, range, and task fulfilment
Maintain extended turns with clear organisation
Speculate, hypothesise, and express degrees of certainty
Negotiate, persuade, and agree/disagree politely
Describe and compare visual information in detail
Develop answers with examples and explanations
Manage turn-taking and interrupt appropriately
